ops.text: PRE-REGISTRATION E43 (NEW, σ_train-independent — a distinct 11-way task; DIRECTLY answers the user's "try small specific models for e.g. categorizing the kind of steer"). Hypothesis: a small fine-tuned encoder can categorize the KIND of steer (the 11-way category: 6 steering + 5 noise) better than the baselines — a task the current pipeline only does via the expensive scaffolded LLM triage judge. Data: the frozen STEER-TYPE eval frame (n=250, 11-way `category`, digest e2a2cb8f; shipped db4c6914) — small, so report macro-F1 + accuracy + per-class F1 (the 11-way confusion is the point; 'other' is near-empty per E30.3, expect it to be unlearnable). Infra: encoder.py (EncoderSpec/train_encoder/score_frozen; adapt to multiclass head — the module is binary-shaped, so a multiclass path may need a thin experiment-local extension, flagged if so). Arms: a fine-tuned small encoder (bert-base or ModernBERT-base multiclass head), a TF-IDF+logreg baseline, a majority-class floor, and — as the incumbent to beat — the scaffolded LLM triage judge's category (the current production way to get "kind of steer"; its category-agreement is E30.3's 0.59 κ / 65.4% raw). PRIMARY: macro-F1 on the frozen test split vs the TF-IDF baseline and vs the LLM-judge incumbent; a bootstrap CI (session-clustered) on the macro-F1 delta. If the small encoder matches the LLM judge's category accuracy at ~0 marginal cost, that is a real "small specific model replaces expensive LLM" win. VARIANCE: 3-seed spread on the encoder. $15 HARD CAP (local encoder training + at most a small LLM-judge scoring pass on the 250 if not already stored). Harness ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E43/ own venv. VERIFY: independent macro-F1 recompute from persisted per-row predictions; split integrity (test untouched in training); the 3-seed spread; incumbent-judge-category provenance. No artifact ships — a win is a "the pipeline could categorize steers with a cheap local encoder" recommendation to the user.
